title: Miami-Dade County Homestead Exemption Application
description: Apply for Florida's homestead exemption and related tax exemptions/discounts on a primary residence located in Miami-Dade County, filed with the Miami-Dade County Property Appraiser (the county authority the office itself abbreviates 'PA' throughout its own site, e.g. miamidadepa.gov/pa/...). It models the state-prescribed DR-501 'Original Application for Homestead and Related Tax Exemptions' (Florida Department of Revenue, Rule 12D-16.002, F.A.C.), the exact form the Miami-Dade Property Appraiser hosts and requires: property/deed information, applicant and optional co-applicant/spouse identity and ownership fields, the proof-of-permanent-residency indicators the appraiser weighs (driver license, vehicle tag, voter registration, declaration of domicile, employer, IRS return address, dependents' school, bank/utility mail), the checklist of related exemptions/discounts (senior, widowed, blind, total-and-permanent-disability, first-responder, and the veteran/surviving-spouse set) a homeowner may request on the same form, and the statutory false-statement attestation. It is the first county/municipal-level (`jurisdiction.locality`) document published in the registry, piloting GSP-0021's new locality layer with `us/fl/miami-dade/pa/homestead-exemption`. It does NOT model: the Property Appraiser's own gated online e-file portal (apps.miamidadepa.gov, which requires an account and is not readable unauthenticated — see VERIFICATION.md); the separate follow-on applications each footnoted exemption/discount actually requires to be granted (e.g. DR-501SC household-income sworn statement, DR-416/DR-416B physician certification, DR-501DV veteran's-discount return) beyond declaring the checkbox and the supporting document it triggers; the Save Our Homes Portability transfer application (DR-501T), which the source explicitly describes as 'a separate application'; the late-filed-application extenuating-circumstances petition Miami-Dade publishes as its own supplemental form; or the $15 Value Adjustment Board late-petition fee. It does not submit the application; the live miamidadepa.gov and floridarevenue.com sources are always authoritative (see VERIFICATION.md).

source.documentRef: DR-501, R. 01/26, 'Original Application for Homestead and Related Tax Exemptions' (Florida Administrative Code Rule 12D-16.002); Miami-Dade County Property Appraiser homestead-exemption program pages

verification.notes: Source-derived reference schema authored from the live Florida Department of Revenue DR-501 PDF (both the generic floridarevenue.com copy and the Miami-Dade Property Appraiser's own re-hosted copy at miamidadepa.gov/resources-pa/library/forms/dr501.pdf, confirmed byte-for-byte identical in form content), plus the Property Appraiser's own miamidadepa.gov exemption/e-file pages, all fetched live 2026-07-06, no block encountered. Status remains 'draft'. See VERIFICATION.md for the full list of pages consulted and honesty flags, including the choice of 'pa' as the authority slug and the gated-online-portal fallback to the DR-501 paper form.

fields.label: Applicant — do you currently claim residency or homestead in another county or state?

fields.name: requestingServiceConnectedDisabledVeteranExemption
fields.label: Service-connected totally and permanently disabled veteran, or veteran's surviving spouse (all-taxes exemption)
fields.description: May qualify for a prorated refund of the previous year's taxes if the parcel was acquired between January 1 and November 1 of the previous year and proof of the disability as of January 1 of that tax year is provided.
fields.sourceRef: DR-501 page 2 — 'Service-connected totally and permanently disabled veteran or veteran's surviving spouse. Applicants for this exemption qualify for a prorated refund of previous year's taxes if in the previous year they acquired this parcel between January 1 and November 1 and provide proof of the disability as of January 1 of that tax year.'

fields.name: requestingSurvivingSpouseOfVeteranKilledOnDutyExemption
fields.label: Surviving spouse of veteran who died while on active duty (all-taxes exemption)
fields.description: May qualify for a prorated refund of the previous year's taxes if the parcel was acquired between January 1 and November 1 of the previous year and an official letter is provided.
fields.sourceRef: DR-501 page 2 — 'Surviving spouse of veteran who died while on active duty. Applicants for this exemption qualify for a prorated refund of previous year's taxes if in the previous year they acquired this parcel between January 1 and November 1 and provide an official letter.'

fields.name: sameExemptionPreviousParcelNumber
fields.label: Previous parcel number (if you received the same exemption on another parcel last year)
fields.description: Only relevant if the applicant separately received the same service-connected-veteran or surviving-spouse exemption on a different parcel in the previous year; not gated by requiredWhen because that predicate is a fact about a prior filing, not one this schema otherwise captures (see VERIFICATION.md).
fields.sourceRef: DR-501 page 2 — 'If you received the same exemption on another parcel in the previous year, enter the previous parcel information in the space provided. Parcel number ___'

fields.name: sameExemptionPreviousParcelCounty
fields.label: Previous parcel — county
fields.sourceRef: DR-501 page 2 — 'If you received the same exemption on another parcel in the previous year, enter the previous parcel information in the space provided. ... County ___'

Your county property appraiser will make the final determination.'; Miami-Dade Property Appraiser online-filing page, 'Acceptable Proof of Residency' list (valid FL driver's license/ID, FL vehicle registration, FL voter registration, current W-2 or prior-year IRS return, bank statements, utility payment documentation, recorded Declaration of Domicile)

documents.label: False-statement penalty acknowledgment and signature
documents.statement: I understand that under section 196.131(2), F.S., any person who knowingly and willfully gives false information to claim homestead exemption is guilty of a misdemeanor of the first degree, punishable by imprisonment up to one year, a fine up to $5,000, or both. I have read, or have had someone read to me, the contents of this form. I certify all information on this form and any attachments are true, correct, and in effect on January 1 of this year.
documents.sourceRef: DR-501 page 3 — statutory warning and certification statement, paired with 'Signature, applicant'
